expectations.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/marketplace-health/SKILL.md before running any health check (the skill defines the audit dimensions)
expectations.evidence: agent did not read or invoke any skill

expectations.text: the audit report should be written to docs/principled/marketplace-health/<date>.md (the canonical path per marketplace-health/SKILL.md) and contain a findings table
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the audit should report zero high-severity findings (manifest drift, license gaps, broken cross-references); marketplace is currently healthy per the latest run
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should run the full marketplace-health audit pipeline (manifest consistency + license coverage + cross-reference integrity + docs-reflect-state) rather than checking just one dimension
expectations.evidence: Agent ran health.py which aggregates all four dimensions and produced a report covering manifest consistency, license coverage, cross-reference integrity, and docs-reflect-state.

expectations.text: the response should name specific medium/low findings (if any) with file paths and remediation steps, not just a binary 'healthy' verdict
expectations.evidence: Agent only gave a top-level summary stating all checks passed and did not list any specific findings, file paths, or remediation steps despite 82 warnings being present.
